name: Carolina Fruitcake
recipeInstructions:
1. Reserve some of the candied fruit and a few pecan halves to decorate the tops of the fruitcakes.
2. Mix flour and spices thoroughly. Then mix the flour mixture with the fruit so that each piece of fruit and each nut is coated lightly with flour. Do this part the first day and set it aside until the next morning.
3. Cream butter (or margarine). Add sugar gradually. Add eggs, mixing thoroughly. Stir in flour and salt until well blended.
4. Pour batter over fruit-nut mixture. Mix with hands until fruit and nuts are well coated with batter.
5. Spread mixture in a greased roasting pan (17 x 11 1/2 x 2 1/4 inches). Bake in preheated moderated oven (350°F) for 30 minutes. Reduce heat to slow (325°F) and continue baking for 1 hour. Watch the heat carefully; do not overbake.
6. After 45 minutes of second baking, remove roaster from oven and quickly stir mixture, breaking up the top crust, scraping sides and bottom of pan. Continue baking 15 minutes. When done, the batter will lose its gloss and be brown and crumbly.
7. Grease pans you wish to use: Loaf pans, muffin pans, coffee cans, decorated tins, etc. Line them with paper baking liners if desired.
8. Spoon hot cake into one pan at a time, making a layer about 3/4-inch thick, pressing firmly. Add another layer and press, repeating until the pan is full. If the batter gets too cold to mold properly, put the roaster pan back in the oven briefly but watch it carefully to avoid overcooking.
9. Decorate while hot with crystallized fruit and nuts. When packed carefully, this cake may be sliced very thin.
10. Optional storage: Cut pieces of clean, washed cloth (not starched) to 16 x 20 inches or another size needed for your cakes. Soak the cloth in brandy (Napoleon or apple). Wrap the cake tightly with the soaked cloth and place it in a ziplock plastic bag, storing airtight. Check periodically and renew the brandy if necessary.
11. Baked at Thanksgiving or around Christmas, these fruitcakes store nicely using the above method. For longer storage, freeze the cakes or store them in brandy-cloth wrappings for up to 6 months.
Yield: Four 9 x 15 x 3-inch loaves, or equivalent in other pans such as mini-bundt, loaf, paper-lined cookie, or coffee cans. These have been given as Christmas gifts and are well received by fruitcake connoisseurs.
